frameworks.name: reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: dopamine as a reward prediction error signal driving learning

frameworks.name: reward prediction error account of interval timing
frameworks.one_line: RPE framework extended to control speed of subjective time

frameworks.name: unified theory of RL and interval timing
frameworks.one_line: single computational account linking dopamine, RPE, and time perception

core_question: Does the timing of dopamine signals relative to reward bidirectionally update the speed of subjective time on subsequent trials?
key_assumptions: dopamine encodes a reward prediction error signal
ramping dopamine slope reads out subjective time speed
early DA signals speed up and late DA signals slow down subjective time
manipulations: reanalysis of dopamine timing across trials in a self-timed movement task
trial-by-trial variation in previous-trial DA signal timing
key_findings: trial-by-trial changes in ramping dopamine slope are predicted by previous-trial DA timing
ramping slope changes are bidirectional, consistent with the RPE-timing prediction
interpretations: dopamine mediates a bidirectional update of interval timing
result supports a unified computational theory linking RL and interval timing
